input.background: Three examples of the combination formula ${ }_n C_x=\frac{n!}{x!(n-x)!}$ are presented.
input.question: How many ways can you choose two items out of three?

output.answer: ${ }_3 C_2=\frac{3!}{2!(3-2)!}=\frac{3 \cdot 2 \cdot 1}{2 \cdot 1(1)}=\frac{6}{2}=3$. This means that you can choose two items out of three three different ways-label items $A, B$, and $C$. You can choose \{A, B\}, \{A, C\}, or \{B, C\}.
